entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop used in the sole cropping system studied for disease incidence.

entities.name: PLANT DENSITY
entities.description: The population of plants per unit area, which was found to inversely affect disease incidence.

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The application of water to plots, which reduced disease incidence compared to nonirrigated plots.

entities.name: PEANUT BUD NECROSIS TOSPOVIRUS (PBNV)
entities.description: The tospovirus responsible for causing peanut bud necrosis disease.

entities.name: DISEASE INCIDENCE
entities.description: The measure of the occurrence of peanut bud necrosis disease in the groundnut crop.

relationships.description: Increased plant density significantly decreased disease incidence.
Irrigation resulted in lower disease incidence compared to nonirrigated plots.
The virus that causes bud necrosis in the groundnut crop.
Plant density affects the incidence of the virus.
Irrigation affects the incidence of the virus.
